program.name: CommBank Graduate, Intern and Analyst Programs
locations: Australia (roles across Australia; metro and regional, depending on stream/career pathway)
Sydney, Melbourne (Technology stream locations vary; and some listed roles in Sydney/Melbourne on applications page)
program.duration_text: Graduate Program: 12–24 months depending on your program length. Summer Intern Program: 10 weeks. Global Markets Summer Analyst Program: 10 weeks.
program.rotation_text: Graduate Program: You will rotate through different areas over the program. Career Transition Program (separate program): 2 x 6 month rotations (not part of Graduate/Intern/Analyst programs page).

eligibility.work_rights: You must be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or an Australian permanent resident at the time of your application. Temporary visa holders are not eligible. Bridging visas while awaiting permanent residency and 820 visa holders are not included.
eligibility.graduation_window: Graduate Program: You must be in your final year at university or have completed studies in the last 24 months. Summer Intern Program and Global Markets Summer Analyst Program: You must be in your second last year of a university undergraduate or postgraduate degree.
minimum_requirements: Overall credit average (65 WAM/GPA). For Technology: Engineering + Cyber Security, minimum is 70 WAM/GPA.
Graduate Program: final year student or completed studies in last 24 months.
Intern/Analyst programs: second last year student.
Must meet citizenship/permanent residency requirements at the time you apply.

stages.name: Apply online
stages.details: Submit an online application form. You will choose an area/career pathway and stream, and you should have your resume and most recent transcript ready.

stages.name: Online Assessment
stages.details: If your application is successful, you will be invited to complete an online assessment and receive insights about your capabilities.

stages.name: Digital Interview
stages.details: You answer short pre-recorded questions using your computer or mobile camera. The email invite includes instructions and a 48-hour deadline.

stages.name: Assessment Centre
stages.details: If you pass the digital interview stage, you will be invited to a virtual or face-to-face assessment centre to meet people from the business and network. Global Markets Summer Analyst candidates may instead complete virtual interviews with teams (and sometimes an extra coding assessment), followed by a second interview round for those who progress.

tips: Select your program option carefully, because if you choose an option that is not listed for that intake, your application will not be considered.
CommBank does not consider multiple applications per candidate.
Have an updated resume and your most recent transcript ready before applying.
Watch your email after the digital interview invite, because you have 48 hours to complete it.

exec_themes: Build “tomorrow’s bank today” by leading in technology, data and AI to improve customer experience and how employees work.
Keep customers safe (especially from scams, fraud, cyber threats and financial crime) and keep the bank “safe and strong” through strong risk management and governance.
Stay at the centre of customers’ financial lives and grow primary relationships across retail and business banking.
Support Australia’s future economy and improve living standards (including through financing and banking services that back long-term national growth).
Embed sustainability (environmental and social factors) into strategy, with a focus on climate transition and transparent reporting.
commercial_context.profit_engine: CommBank’s profit engine is a large, customer-focused banking franchise that earns income from core banking products (like home lending, everyday banking and business banking) and aims to sustain returns by (1) growing and deepening customer relationships, (2) investing in technology/AI to improve service and efficiency, and (3) maintaining strong risk and capital settings so it can keep lending through different economic conditions.
commercial_context.headwinds: CBA operates in a highly regulated industry where maintaining stakeholder trust is critical, and it faces ongoing threats from scams, fraud, cyber threats and financial crime that require continuous investment in detection, prevention and response capabilities. The bank also treats AI as a material risk type that needs governance, controls and ongoing review as technology and regulatory guidance evolve.
commercial_context.esg: CommBank says it is embedding environmental and social matters into its strategy and is committed to supporting Australia’s transition to a more prosperous, resilient and sustainable future. Key ESG focus areas highlighted include climate commitment (including progressing disclosures to meet mandatory climate reporting), customer advocacy and accessibility, cyber security/scams/fraud, supplier diversity, community initiatives, Indigenous rights and reconciliation, and inclusion and diversity. In its AI governance, it also states AI principles that cover environmental and social impacts, fairness, transparency, privacy/data protection, reliability/security, and accountability.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: CommBank is scaling and formalising its AI approach, including publishing a dedicated “Our Approach to Adopting AI” report (accurate as at 31 December 2025) and explicitly treating AI as a material risk with specific governance forums (e.g., an AI Risk Committee) and a Group AI Policy framework. It also describes shifting its internal AI learning toward more experiential, hands-on training (noted as a July 2025 next phase).

notes: The page lists multiple programs (Graduate, Summer Intern, Global Markets Summer Analyst, Technology Associate).
The site provides application timing as months (for example, 'March and July'), not exact opening/closing days. Because no year and no specific day are given, dates cannot be converted to YYYY-MM-DD.
The page also states that applications are currently closed, but it does not provide a closing date.

sources.title: Our Graduate and Intern Programs - CommBank

notes: Work-rights and study-timing eligibility is explicitly stated for each program on the Graduate & Intern Programs page.
The applications details page adds guidance about choosing options and multiple applications, but does not add clearer work-rights wording than the programs page in the snippet we used.

sources.title: Our Graduate and Intern Programs - CommBank

notes: The recruitment stages (apply online, online assessment, digital interview, assessment centre) are described on the Graduate & Intern Programs page.
Global Markets Summer Analyst differences are described on the same page.

sources.title: Our Graduate and Intern Programs - CommBank

notes: Most themes come directly from CommBank’s 2025 Annual Report landing page (strategic priorities headings and summaries).

The profit engine is summarised at a high level from strategy and performance descriptions on the Annual Report page, but detailed revenue mix (e.g., net interest vs fees) is not explicitly stated in the extracted lines; treat this as a plain-English interpretation of the bank’s stated strategy rather than a quantified breakdown.

Headwinds are captured mainly from CommBank’s own framing of regulatory context, trust, scam/fraud/cyber threats, and AI risk governance (AI treated as a material risk). It does not quantify cost or impact in the cited sources.
ESG content is based on the Sustainability page plus the Annual Report sustainability/climate sections list, and the AI report’s stated principles (including environmental and social impacts).

“Recent pivot” is based on dated milestones stated in the AI report (as-at date 31 Dec 2025; training phase shift in July 2025) and the Annual Report’s emphasis on technology/AI. It does not claim a sudden strategic reversal—rather, a clearer, more formalised AI program and governance.
